replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market ranges for the AJ34/AJ34S engine. Reconditioned units are available from specialist Jaguar engine rebuilders. Prices range from £3,000 to £6,000 on average . A rebuilt 4.2 AJ-V8 engine typically starts from £3,500 . All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an XJ 4.2 V8 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent Jaguar specialist) or £140–£190/hr (London/SE dealer). The V8 is well-packaged in the XJ's aluminium body.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative replacement before failure — always cheaper than engine replacement
Replace as a set — common on higher-mileage examples
Replacement often £4,000–£7,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Naturally aspirated XJ8 — the 4.2 is the engine to buy, more reliable than the 3.6
* Documented timing chain tensioner history (especially on higher-mileage examples)
* Cooling system proactively replaced (water pump, thermostat, hoses)
* Full service history with regular oil changes (7,500–10,000 miles)
* XJR with documented supercharger coupler history
avoidIf: * Any evidence of cold-start rattle — timing chain tensioner wear is serious
* Coolant loss or overheating without documented repair — cooling system risk
* Poor service history — the AJ-V8 is robust but needs proper maintenance
* XJR with supercharger rattle without documented repair

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X350/X358 XJ with the 4.2 V8 is one of the best modern Jaguars you can buy. The 4.2 is the engine to choose — the 3.6 has well-documented chain and tensioner problems, but the 4.2 is robust and silky. Naturally aspirated XJ8s are the most reliable; the XJR adds supercharger complexity but rewards with 400bhp. Buy on history, maintain the cooling system, and the 4.2 V8 will serve you well.

cta.label: → Compare Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain tensioner wear warning)
* Service History — 7,500–10,000 mile oil changes are essential for the AJ-V8
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — cooling system components are a known weakness
* Engine Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation, flat spots, or rough idle?
* Supercharger Health (XJR) — Any rattling from the supercharger at idle? (coupler wear)
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve covers; oil leaks can develop on higher-mileage engines
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 engine (AJ34/AJ34S) typically costs £4,000–£7,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. A rebuilt 4.2 AJ-V8 engine starts from around £3,500. The supercharged XJR variant typically commands a premium. Check the Era Map above for your specific engine code.

items.question: Is the Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 reliable?
items.answer: Yes — the 4.2 V8 is widely regarded as the engine to buy in the X350/X358 XJ. As one owner review states: "The 4.2 is the only engine to buy — the 3.6 V8 has chain and tensioner problems, the 4.2 has none of them". The engine typically lasts beyond 150,000 miles with proper maintenance. Key watchpoints are timing chain tensioners after 100,000 miles and coolant system components prone to leaks. Regular oil changes (7,500–10,000 miles) are essential.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner wear (rattling on cold start, £2,000–£4,500), cooling system leaks (water pump, thermostat, coolant pipes, £400–£1,200), and supercharger coupler wear on XJR models (rattling at idle, £500–£1,000). The naturally aspirated 4.2 is considered more reliable than the supercharged version.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: XJ values range from £3,000–£15,000 depending on condition, mileage and specification. A £4,000–£7,000 reconditioned engine represents 25–80% of vehicle value — our 60% Jaguar scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. The XJR (supercharged) is generally more valuable and worth the investment.

items.question: How do I find my Jaguar XJ 4.2 V8 engine code?
items.answer: The XJ 4.2 V8 has used two engine variants: AJ34 (naturally aspirated, 300bhp, XJ8) and AJ34S (supercharged, 400bhp, XJR). The naturally aspirated engine has a compression ratio of 11.0:1, while the supercharged version is 9.1:1. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the timing chain tensioner issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and tensioner replacement costs £2,000–£4,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£4,000–£7,000). A timing chain kit including chains, tensioners, guides and blades is available. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic engine failure.
